title: dalea cinnamomea
intro: Dalea cinnamomea, also known as Cinnamon Senna or Cinnamon Prickly Poppy, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Dalea cinnamomea include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Dalea cinnamomea include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although its use should be consulted with a healthcare professional.
